title: B-minimal: frozen round-0 rerun to make H1 a clean causal finding
target: isolate the loop's incremental effect; H2 stays honest pilot; autopsy stays headline

meta.text: does NOT fix H2 power (that's B-full)

intent: GPT's valid core hit: because each arm draws its own round-0, H1 compares two independently-sampled pipelines, not the loop's isolated effect. B-minimal fixes exactly that: generate one round-0 Tester suite per replicate, FREEZE it, and run all three continuations (no-critic / same-lineage critic / cross-lineage critic) from that identical frozen state. Repeat over K independent round-0 draws per subject to average out the huge draw variance (you saw 69/168/50/62/65 on one subject). H1 becomes a within-replicate paired comparison on the identical starting suite -- a true causal isolation. The hard 80% (engine, providers, receipts, the fixed fail-loud instrument, analysis scaffold) already exists and is NOT rebuilt. H2's ceiling/power problem is out of scope (needs new harder subjects = B-full); it stays a pilot observation, and the autopsy stays the paper's contribution.

steps.label: Re-pre-register the frozen-round-0 design (commit protocol vN BEFORE any paid run)
Harness change: freeze round-0 suite + inject it as the seed for all 3 continuations; add replicate loop
Free model-free dry-run validation (validate_scopes-style, $0) before spending
Run K replicates x subjects (one frozen round-0 shared across the 3 continuations)
New analysis: per-replicate paired kill-delta + subject-clustered effect sizes & intervals (not pooled mutant McNemar)
Revise paper: H1 now causal, + fold GPT's other valid fixes (tone, surveys, cost-3.4x, power language, temper pre-reg) -> Fable + optional GPT re-review

decision.question: "How many replicates (K) and which subjects -- the scope knob that sets cost and power?"

rejected.label: K=5 on all 5 subjects
rejected.detail: 25 replicates x 3 continuations = ~75 runs, ~$30-45, includes the deadlock-prone attrition subject; more power but more cost + flake risk

chosen.label: K=5 on the 4 clean subjects (drop flaky attrition)
chosen.detail: 20 replicates x 3 = ~60 runs, ~$15-30; matches the ~5-draw variance you already observed; sidesteps the attrition deadlock; H1 stats span the same 4 subjects the paper already reports

decision.verdict: Recommend K=5 on the 4 clean subjects for B-minimal; attrition (the degenerate + deadlock-prone one) is where the least is gained and the most can go wrong. Your call on K (3 = cheaper/less power, 5 = the sweet spot) and whether to keep attrition.

roles: K x subjects x 3 receipted cells
Opus drafts, Fable/GPT review, Jeff locks

artifacts.label: crucible.loop / crucible.experiment -- seed-suite injection + replicate loop
experiments/runs -- fresh receipted cells (K x subjects x 3)
paper Results/Method/Discussion -- H1 causal + GPT's valid fixes
engine, providers, fixed fail-loud instrument, subjects -- UNTOUCHED, reused

risks.body: Doing this RIGHT means H1's effect could come back SMALLER than 105-0, because you're now measuring the loop's true incremental effect, not the confounded one. That is the honest point of the exercise -- but be ready for a more modest H1. (The observed c=0 pattern suggests the loop still dominates, but a clean design can shrink it.)
risks.ask: Comfortable that a causally-clean H1 might be a smaller, honest effect?

risks.body: attrition-risk-ml is the deadlock-prone subject that ate 4 failed rerun attempts last time. Including it risks re-suffering that. Recommended: drop it from B-minimal.
risks.ask: Keep attrition, or run B-minimal on the 4 clean subjects?

risks.body: B-minimal does NOT resolve H2 (ceiling/power). H2 stays an honest underpowered pilot; the frozen-round-0 rerun for a REAL H2 (more, harder subjects) is B-full, deliberately out of scope here.
Scope-creep pull toward B-full (adding subjects to chase H2 power). Hold the line unless you decide to commit to the bigger study.
